dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8983 IH: Practitioner Enforcement and Tracking of Substances Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Mills introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To increase compliance with PDMP requirements by veterinarians, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Practitioner Enforcement and Tracking of Substances Act or the PETS Act.

paragraph.text: Section 399O(h)(1) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 280g–3(h)(1)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking Nothing in this section and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: Except as specified in paragraph (2), nothing in this section

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:
Paragraph (1) shall not apply with respect to the authority of a State to prohibit reporting to the PDMP of the dispensing of a controlled substance to a minor patient, if—

clause.text: the prescribing or dispensing practitioner is a veterinarian licensed in such State; and
the patient to whom the controlled substance is prescribed or dispensed is a veterinary patient.

subparagraph.text: A veterinarian described in subparagraph (A) shall report to the PDMP, in lieu of information required under this section relating to the veterinary patient involved, such information relating to the owner or primary caretaker of the veterinary patient.

paragraph.text: Section 399O(k)(3) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 280g–3(k)(3)) is amended by striking physician, and inserting physician, veterinarian,.

It is the sense of Congress that, notwithstanding State law, a veterinarian is required to comply with the requirements of section 399O of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 280g–3) with respect to consulting the PDMP (as defined in section 399O(k) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 280g–3(k))) prior to prescribing any controlled substance, and reporting any dispensing of a controlled substance to the PDMP to the same extent as any other practitioner (as defined in subsection (k) of such section) is required to comply with such requirements.

subsection.text: Section 399O(b) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 280g–3(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (5), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (6), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

the reporting to the PDMP by each prescribing or dispensing practitioner, or their designee, of any instance of a non-fatal overdose event that required intervention by such practitioner (or designee), not later than 72 hours after such overdose event occurred.

subsection.text: Section 399O(c)(2) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 280g–3(c)(2)) is amended by striking may and inserting shall.
